codeReview.summary: Rate limiting implementation is complete and correct. The middleware is properly configured with 100 requests per 15 minutes per IP, applied to all /api/* routes, and the health endpoint is correctly excluded. Tests cover all validation contract assertions: middleware import, header presence, 429 response on limit exceeded, and health endpoint exclusion.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The backend-worker skill instructs to install '@hono/rate-limiter' but the actual package name is 'hono-rate-limiter' (without @hono/ scope). The worker discovered this through web search and correctly installed the right package. The skill should be updated to reflect the correct package name.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Skill at .factory/skills/backend-worker/SKILL.md says: 'Install: `bun add @hono/rate-limiter`'. The actual package installed is 'hono-rate-limiter@0.5.3' per package.json. Worker documented this in skillFeedback.suggestedChanges: 'Consider updating the backend-worker skill to mention hono-rate-limiter instead of @hono/rate-limiter as the package name has changed'

sharedStateObservations.observation: The validation contract VAL-QUAL-015 specifies '@hono/rate-limiter' but the actual package is 'hono-rate-limiter'. The validation contract should be updated to match reality.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: validation-contract.md VAL-QUAL-015 says 'package.json contains @hono/rate-limiter' but package.json actually contains 'hono-rate-limiter'. The feature fulfills the intent (rate limiting is installed) but the package name differs from documentation.

summary: PASS - Rate limiting implementation meets all validation contract requirements. The middleware is correctly configured and applied to API routes, health endpoint is excluded, tests verify all assertions. Minor documentation gaps: both the skill and validation contract reference incorrect package name '@hono/rate-limiter' instead of actual 'hono-rate-limiter'.
